reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Adjudication covered ten accumulated findings across provider bootstrap, reindex cancellation, TCP transport, workflow locks, daemon traceability ledgers, model-server locks/listeners, socket reclaim, file-matrix drift, and env reference drift.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected only the files cited by the ten accumulated findings plus directly relevant counter-evidence locations from prior deltas; no new discovery sweep was performed.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-001-P1-001 evidence still supports explicit provider override being bypassed during fresh bootstrap.

searchActions.result: ensureActiveEmbedder persists an auto-selected provider before startup provider config resolution; factory still gives explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER precedence.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-001-P1-002 evidence still supports running cancellation being ignored by the worker.

searchActions.result: cancelJob marks non-terminal jobs cancelled; runJob loops and completes without rereading status.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-002-P1-001 evidence still supports unauthenticated non-loopback TCP exposure.

searchActions.result: TCP/http targets remain accepted without loopback/auth guards; /api/embed accepts POSTed input strings without token checks.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-002-P1-002 evidence still supports fail-open workflow locks.

searchActions.result: Both global and per-folder lock acquisition can return false, and callers still run the operation regardless.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-003-P1-001 evidence still supports conflicting daemon child completion ledgers.

searchActions.result: Open verification rows remain in tasks/checklists while implementation summaries/spec status claim implemented and some checks PASS.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds as release traceability debt; no downgrade.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-005-P1-001 evidence still supports live-owner respawn lock age expiry.

searchActions.result: The lock stale predicate still age-expires locks after 60000ms while comments say the demand lock is intentionally held for the listener lifetime.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-006-P1-001 evidence still supports demand-spawn failure stranding the lazy listener.

searchActions.result: Demand handler releases listener before launch; launch can return false on no PID; lock release is not in a finally-protected block.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-001-P2-001 evidence still supports weaker direct socket reclaim semantics.

searchActions.result: Direct binary still unlinks Unix socket on EADDRINUSE; supervised listener refuses to reclaim when live resident PID is recorded.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; final severity remains advisory P2.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-003-P2-001 evidence still supports phase 005 file-matrix drift.

searchActions.queryOrPath: phase 005 spec matrix and implementation summary/code
searchActions.result: Spec still assigns idle eviction to hf-model-server.cjs; summary and code identify model-server-supervision.cjs.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; final severity remains advisory P2.

searchLedger.hypothesis: DR-004-P2-001 evidence still supports env reference drift.

searchActions.result: Prefix overrides, reindex batch size, and HF_LOCAL_MODEL are code-visible; the embedding env table still omits them.

searchLedger.rationale: Evidence holds; final severity remains advisory P2.

claim: Fresh daemon bootstrap can ignore an explicit EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER override by calling ensureActiveEmbedder before resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig and persisting an auto-selected local-first active embedder.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether ensureActiveEmbedder receives the explicit provider or resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig runs before active shard attachment; neither is true in the current files.
alternativeExplanation: A best-available active pointer could be intended for auto mode, but it conflicts with explicit provider precedence when EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER is non-auto.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if startup is changed so explicit provider config runs before any active embedder persistence or if explicit overrides are documented to be ignored for active metadata bootstrap.

claim: Cancelling a running embedder reindex does not stop the worker before it writes vectors and completes the active embedder switch.
counterevidenceSought: Checked for an in-loop status reread, cancellation token, abort path, and running-cancel test; the current worker only checks terminal status before starting.
alternativeExplanation: Cancellation might be intended only for queued jobs, but cancelJob accepts any non-terminal job including running.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the public contract is changed so running jobs cannot be cancelled and UI/API callers cannot request cancellation for running jobs.

claim: TCP model-server targets can expose unauthenticated embed traffic outside loopback.
counterevidenceSought: Checked for host allowlisting, loopback-only enforcement, bearer-token checks, and an explicit authenticated remote TCP contract; current code accepts tcp/http targets and POST /api/embed without auth.
alternativeExplanation: Remote TCP may be an intentional operator escape hatch, but that needs explicit opt-in and authentication because embedding inputs can contain private memory/spec text.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if remote unauthenticated TCP is documented as a supported non-sensitive deployment mode, or if TCP is restricted to loopback/authenticated transport by default.

claim: Workflow locks fail open after timeout or unexpected acquisition errors, re-admitting concurrent standalone writers.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether callers abort when the lock acquisition result is false; they still execute operation() in both global and per-folder lock wrappers.
alternativeExplanation: The in-process queue serializes same-process calls, but it does not protect separate standalone generate-context.js processes.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if direct standalone indexing/writes become impossible under daemon-down concurrency or if lock acquisition failure becomes a hard error for write phases.

claim: Daemon child packets leave required verification gates open while claiming implementation/completion status, making the release ledger contradictory.
counterevidenceSought: Checked implementation summaries, tasks, and checklists for reconciled evidence. Some summaries claim PASS or implemented status, while task/checklist verification rows remain open or pending.
alternativeExplanation: The implementation summaries may be newer than task/checklist ledgers, but the packet still lacks a single authoritative completion ledger.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if task/checklist files are explicitly declared non-authoritative for release readiness or reconciled with evidence/defer notes.

claim: Lazy model-server respawn locks expire while the live listener still owns them.
counterevidenceSought: Checked release sites and current stale predicate behavior. The predicate still age-expires locks after 60000ms without requiring the owner PID to be dead.
alternativeExplanation: Age-based expiry is valid for orphaned short critical sections, but the same lock is intentionally held across a long-lived idle listener window.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if another guard proves a second launcher cannot reach acquireDemandRespawnLock after 60 seconds while the first listener remains alive.

claim: Demand-triggered model-server spawn failures can strand the lazy listener after the demand handler tears it down before confirming launch success.
counterevidenceSought: Checked for a try/finally around launch, failed-launch re-arm path, and test coverage for no-PID/synchronous spawn failure; none appeared in the reviewed ranges.
alternativeExplanation: Default spawn failure may be rare with process.execPath, but launch explicitly handles a no-PID child and the demand path still reports loading after listener teardown.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if supported runtimes guarantee spawnFn cannot return a no-PID child or throw synchronously, or if another production path immediately re-arms the demand listener after launched:false.

claim: Direct hf-model-server startup can unlink a live Unix socket without the launcher guard.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the direct binary has the same recorded-PID/live-resident guard as supervision; it still unlinks on Unix EADDRINUSE before retrying.
alternativeExplanation: Direct binary use may be an operator-only path and less common than launcher-supervised startup, limiting blast radius.
downgradeTrigger: Rule out if direct startup is made unsupported for shared sockets or gains the supervised live-resident guard.

claim: Phase 005 file-change matrix assigns idle eviction to hf-model-server.cjs even though implementation lives in model-server-supervision.cjs.
counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the spec matrix had been corrected or whether hf-model-server.cjs now owns idle eviction; the matrix still points to the old location while summary/code point to supervision.
alternativeExplanation: The implementation summary documents the final design, so the drift is localized documentation debt rather than behavior risk.
downgradeTrigger: Rule out when the phase 005 file matrix is updated to match the implemented control-layer location.

claim: Several embedding env knobs are code/test-visible but absent from ENV_REFERENCE.
counterevidenceSought: Checked the embedding env table and cascade-probe table for HF_EMBEDDINGS_PREFIX_DOC, HF_EMBEDDINGS_PREFIX_QUERY, EMBEDDER_REINDEX_BATCH_SIZE, and HF_LOCAL_MODEL; the table still omits them.
alternativeExplanation: HF_LOCAL_MODEL may be an undocumented compatibility alias rather than a supported knob, but the prefix overrides and reindex batch size are code/test-visible operator controls.
downgradeTrigger: Rule out once intentional knobs are documented and any unsupported alias is removed or explicitly marked internal.

summary: All active P1/P2 claims were verified against current cited files; no accumulated finding was downgraded or ruled out.
